content:
Anti-pattern: positive tab order

Avoid hardcoding the focus sequence. Markup like <nav tabindex="2"> forces a custom order that breaks Shift+Tab. Prefer source order instead.
note: ceiling / FP-exposer: regex fires on non-flaggable text. MDN Web Docs and the WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices both document the positive-tabindex anti-pattern with the value escaped as HTML entities (
ceiling / FP-exposer: regex fires on non-flaggable text. The eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y rule no-noninteractive-tabindex ships docstrings and rule descriptions that quote the offending pattern verbatim
ceiling / FP-exposer: regex fires on non-flaggable text. Accessibility linters such as axe-core and Lighthouse store human-readable violation messages as string constants that quote the bad value (
ceiling / FP-exposer: regex fires on non-flaggable text. Documentation generators (Docusaurus, VitePress, Prism/highlight.js) render fenced code blocks as escaped HTML inside <pre><code>.
ceiling / FP-exposer: regex fires on non-flaggable text. Detector and linter test suites (Jest/Vitest idiom) embed the offending markup as a string fixture to assert the rule fires.
ceiling / FP-exposer: regex fires on non-flaggable text. JSDoc/TSDoc convention places @deprecated warnings and @example tags that quote the discouraged value (tabindex="7") above the function.

content:
<form action="/checkout" method="post">
  <label for="card">Card number</label>
  <input type="text" id="card" name="card" tabindex="1">
  <label for="exp">Expiry</label>
  <input type="text" id="exp" name="exp" tabindex="2">
  <label for="cvc">CVC</label>
  <input type="text" id="cvc" name="cvc" tabindex="3">
  <button type="submit" tabindex="4">Pay</button>
</form>
note: anchor / TP anchor: detector correctly fires. Classic hand-authored checkout form pattern where developers manually sequence inputs with tabindex 1..4 — a widely documented anti-pattern
